translated: All right, let's go through some of your messages.
First, we have a message from King-san.
Thank you.
Nakashima-san, Kon-needo.
Kon-needo.
In this story, seeing Leoni grow so much as professionals...
...made me really happy.
Both the solo live and the fan festa are chances for Leoni to take a big step forward.
Whether they should write a new song... since there's no right answer, it's a tough choice.
I'm bad at making decisions and often get stuck overthinking things...
...so I really empathized with Honami's feelings.
By the way, Nakashima-san, how do you usually reach a conclusion when making a major decision?
Thanks for writing in.
Thank you.
Hmm...
I'm the type who usually has a clear answer, but in a situation like this story...
...if I wanted to do both, I'd just do both.
But if the jobs are on the same day and I have to choose one...
...well, if it's like this time where the deadlines just overlap...
...and I'm like, "What do I do?" I'd probably just take it on.
So it really depends on what the decision is...
...but I'm generally the type to decide things quickly.
Other than that, I just leave it to my emotions.
So I'm not a very good reference, sorry!
What about all of you?
On the flip side.
Yeah, if I can do both, I want to do both.
I know that's selfish of me.
Right.
Exactly.
Leave it to your emotions.
Yeah.
Someone said, "Drawing lots is the only way."
True.
Well, if you'll have no regrets either way...
...and you really can't decide, then leaving it to luck might be a good idea.
I think trying to decide that way is fine too.
But if I really could only do one...
...I'd go with my gut.
Whichever way I'm leaning more.
If I'm leaning even a few percent more toward one, I'd choose that.
Yeah.
But regarding the story this time...
...since the fan festa offer was withdrawn for now...
...and moved to another time, I'd actually feel relieved.
I might think, "Oh, maybe I can do both now!" I probably shouldn't think that way...
...but I'd probably think I was lucky.
Yeah, something like that.
All right, I'd like to share one more.
Next is from Kopi-san.
Thank you.
Yukky, Kon-needo.
Kon-needo.
I got so excited that Leoni's long-awaited first solo live as professionals is finally happening.
Personally, I'd love to see them do an MC segment where they swap instruments during the actual show.
Thanks for writing in.
Thank you.
I'd definitely want to see that.
Hmm...
But I might hate it.
If I were in their shoes, I'd be so nervous.
Like, how should I put it?
If Shiho played drums, guitar, or keyboard instead of bass...
...I wonder if she'd be okay with that.
They did it because it was a rehearsal, but since she's so good at music...
...and has so much confidence, her pride might get in the way...
...and she might say she doesn't want to do it for an MC segment.
But I still want to see it.
I want to see them all flustered.
Yeah, I want to see them all saying they can't do it.
I feel like I could listen to that and watch it forever.
Yeah.
I hope they do it.
I wonder what they'll do.
I'm looking forward to the future stories.
Someone said, "I can't imagine Shiho-chan on drums."
Ah.
Well, that's true.
But she seems good at keeping the rhythm, so she might handle it well...
...but since your hands and feet have to move independently...
